channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Critique] Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
item.description: A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Ransomware Negotiator Gets 70 Months in Prison for Aiding BlackCat Attacks
item.description: A 41-year-old former ransomware negotiator has been sentenced to nearly six years (i.e., 70 months) in prison in the U.S. for their role in conspiring with the now-defunct BlackCat ransomware operators to extort…

item.title: [Moyen] Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain $3.1 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Security firm Coinspect has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. The flaw is in how some wallet software generated its recovery phrase, the words that…

item.title: [Élevé] Injective SDK on npm infected with cryptocurrency wallet stealer
item.description: Hackers compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and used it to publish a malicious package on the Node Package Manager (npm) that stole cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed…

item.title: [Faible] OpenMandriva Linux says contributor tried to sabotage the project
item.description: The OpenMandriva Linux project announced that it was the target of an attempted act of internal sabotage after a dispute among contributors. [...]

item.title: [Critique] New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as commands the operator can…

item.title: [Élevé] Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description:
Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API.
"Operators rely on…

item.title: [Faible] FFE : une nouvelle fuite massive à la veille des Championnats de France
item.description: Une revendication attribuée à un pirate vise la Fédération française d’équitation, avec des données exposées et un message politique assumé en plein Championnats de France d’équitation.

item.title: [Faible] Mount Royal University confirms breach as hackers claim attack
item.description: Mount Royal University in Calgary says hackers stole and then deleted data from its file storage systems after breaching the university's network. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Microsoft patches RoguePlanet Defender zero-day vulnerability
item.description: Microsoft has released a security patch to address a Defender zero-day vulnerability known as "RoguePlanet," disclosed after the June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] AssuranceAmerica data breach exposes records of 6.9 million drivers
item.description: American insurance company AssuranceAmerica has disclosed a data breach impacting nearly 7 million drivers after attackers gained access to its systems earlier this year. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Police arrests 5,800 suspects in global anti-fraud crackdown
item.description: Law enforcement agencies have arrested 5,811 suspects and seized $293 million in illicit assets in a global anti-fraud operation spanning 97 countries. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft to retire the OWA Light client in Exchange Server
item.description: Microsoft has announced plans to disable Outlook Web Access (OWA) Light, the lightweight version of the Outlook Web App email client, in a future Exchange Server update. [...]

item.title: [Faible] The Hidden Security Risks of Reduced Summer IT Coverage
item.description: Security operations don't slow down when IT teams take vacation, but staffing levels often do. Kaseya explains how AI-driven automation can help organizations maintain consistent security operations and reduce reliance…

item.title: [Faible] New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 focuses on stealing Microsoft 365 accounts by combining adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code methods with AI-assisted lure generation. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft expects more Windows security updates from AI-discovered flaws
item.description: Microsoft says Windows users should expect to see an increase in security updates as the company increasingly relies on artificial intelligence to discover vulnerabilities in its codebase. [...]

item.title: [Faible] New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
item.description: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments.…

item.title: [Faible] Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure comprising more than 230…

item.title: [Faible] GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
item.description: Researchers at Wiz found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. The assistant asks permission to edit one…

item.title: [Critique] Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
item.description:
Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead.
That is the finding in a proof-of-concept published Wednesday by the AI…

item.title: [Faible] Meta's New AI Image Tool Lets Others Use Your Public Instagram Photos in AI Images
item.description:
Meta has announced that its new artificial intelligence (AI) model Muse Image lets people use public Instagram posts and reels to generate AI content, and it's enabled by default.
"You can also @-mention Instagram…

item.title: [Moyen] Microsoft Patches RoguePlanet Defender Flaw That Can Grant SYSTEM Privileges
item.description:
Microsoft has released security updates for a Defender vulnerability known as RoguePlanet, nearly a month after details of the flaw became public.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), is a…

item.title: [Critique] GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy.
According to a new report…

item.title: [Faible] Summer of Clearinghouses
item.description: Everyone seems to have announced a clearinghouse over the past few weeks. We did too. Ours is called Athena, and the main thing that sets it apart is that it was already real and running when we announced it — built…

item.title: [Faible] AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
item.description: AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to the next host before your…

item.title: [Faible] ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
item.description:
Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch it.
This week is full of that kind of damage. Not…

item.title: [Faible] npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description:
GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA).
The…
